title: EphemeralUnavailable sent immediately on AuthFailed (no counter)
description: Auth failures are permanent by protocol definition, so EphemeralUnavailable fires immediately without waiting for N consecutive failures. A future contributor might try to unify this with the StreamEnded counter path (treating auth failure as retryable). That would be wrong: a 401/403 on WS handshake is a server-side rejection of the credentials, not a transient network event. The asymmetry is correct and should be defended.
